24. The Sangguniang Bayan, upon review and deliberation, institutionalize the policy and procedures in engaging and managing COS and JO personnel, as prepared by the HRMO, by legislating it into an ordinance;

We recommended the following:
a.	The Municipal Treasurer to request copies of Credit Memos and Debit Memos from the bank, retrieve the DVs of the unrecorded check issued, and furnish the aforementioned documents to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis to record the reconciling items;
b.	The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer reconcile regularly their respective cash records together with the records of the depository banks pertaining to the cash accounts of the agency to detect instantly errors or fraud either by the bank, Accounting unit or Cash unit; and
c.	The Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to record in the books of accounts the reconciling items upon receipt of the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Determine the status of settlement or collectability of these accounts, exhaust all possible means to collect the receivables including the sending of demand letters; and
Consider the submission of request for write-off, if warranted, following the procedures and qualifications provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended to Management the following:
Ensure regular monitoring on the implementation of projects by the Municipal Engineering Office and assess the feasibility of project implementations;
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to continue reviewing the previous years’ AIPs, evaluate the feasibility of unimplemented PPAs, and propose to the Municipal Planning and Development Council to consider changes or realignments in the budget for projects which cannot be implemented or have residual balances. These funds be redirected to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan; and
Ensure that PPAs to be funded out of the 20 per cent DF are well-planned and procurement-and -implementation-ready.
We recommended that Management formulate strategies in the cost-cutting of expenditures for the operation of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the MDRRM Council, and the MDRRM Officer:
Maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to effectively increase the level of preparedness of the municipality during natural calamities; and
Provide specific timelines and implement the PPAs under the LDRRMFIP within the said timelines.

We recommended that Management include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the STF in the preparation of the LDRRMFIP which will be included in the AIP as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002. In the event that these activities were not initially included in the LDRRMFIP and AIP for the year, prepare and submit a revised LDRRMFIP as recommended for approval by the LDRRMC.

We recommended that Management undertake the following:
Consider disposing the unserviceable PPEs following the guidelines in COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2024-1;
Create disposal team who will take charge in the preparation of required documents, reconcile records with the Accounting Office and ensure that schedules are complete of property descriptions, conduct of inspection, compute appraised value to be used as the floor price for public auction: and
Upon actual disposal, the Municipal Accountant to derecognize these unserviceable properties from the books using the IIRUP.

We recommended that Management, on regular basis:
a) 	Provide the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of the year with a list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year; and
b) 	Inform the Office of the Auditor within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Furnish the Office of the Auditor at the beginning of every calendar year a list of all its on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Inform the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
Prepare and maintain a complete monthly Report of Publicized Government Projects/Programs/Activities containing not only those PPAs procured thru competitive bidding but also those that are procured thru other alternative modes of procurement.

Recommendation
We recommended that the procuring entity furnish a copy of the contract within five (5) days upon perfection to the COA Office as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents and such other documents peculiar to the contract which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommended that the MMO, MSWDO and MAO submit the RDS and other supporting documents to the Audit Team to establish the regularity of the transactions and furnish a copy of the same to the Office of the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the recording of appropriate entries and derecognition of the inventory accounts in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that management direct:

The Municipal Accountant to make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance of the payable accounts and to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments; and

The Municipal Accountant to set up liabilities in accordance with the definitions and recognition criteria set out in PPSASs.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to

Send confirmation letter to the supposed suppliers/payee of the stale check to verify the existence of the obligations and secure sufficient evidence to support the validity of the claims and repayment of the accounts;

Revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no valid claims; and

Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the payable accounts so that this account will consist only of valid claims against the LGU and provide a reliable schedule.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, as applicable, and carefully review and record financial transactions using the appropriate accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review the supporting documents pertaining to claims for transportation and travelling expenses incurred by COS and JO personnel and ensure that such are supported with the additional requirements prescribed in Item B.5 of COA Resolution No. 2021-044.

We recommended that management seek the required prior approval of the Sangguniang Bayan in relation to the implementation of projects charged against lump sum appropriations which are described in generic terms otherwise the projects/programs/activities in the AIP must be described with particularity to facilitate implementation.

We recommended that management ensure that brand names, or if no brand name is available state “no brand” but the detailed description of the item/s to be procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended the following to management:

The Municipal Budget Officer require that the Obligation Requests be prepared by the Requesting Unit and certify the existence of available appropriations for claims to be funded out of the General Fund as provided in COA Circular 2006-002 dated January 31, 2006; and

The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant ensure that all disbursement funded out of the General Fund be in accordance with approved appropriations in compliance with Section 138(a) of GAAM, Volume 1.

We recommended that management direct the LDRRM Officer and the MDRRMC to monitor the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund within the targeted timelines.

We recommended that the LGU GFPS adopt a system that will ensure close monitoring of the execution of all PPAs to warrant their efficient and timely completion in order to achieve the desirable outcomes for the general welfare targeting gender issues and concerns. This is also to ensure that GAD programs are implemented with the use of funds specifically appropriated for GAD projects.
We recommended that management update the GAD budget attributed cost based on the final appropriation at year-end to determine the actual percentage of compliance with GAD budget requirement.

We recommended that the Local School Board maximize the utilization of the SEF through implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget, incorporate allowable expenses to support the BE-LCP as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020. Further for both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile discrepancies noted in the reports they prepared and submitted.

a.	The Local Chief Executive require the conduct of semestral inventory taking and submission of the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively, for verification and review;

b.	The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer acting as the General Services Officer prepare and maintain complete Supply Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of their records, and update the same based on the results of the physical inventory;

c.	The Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant examine the existence of the reported inventory accounts, prepare and submit the corresponding SSMI, as basis for the recording of the necessary adjustments by the latter;
Make the necessary adjustment in the books to reflect the correct balances of the inventory accounts.

c.	Require the inventory committee to prepare and submit the approved PIP to the Office of the Auditor at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory count and perform the count on all PPE items as indicated in the PIP.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the actual amounts presented in the SCBAA and the actual amounts presented in the financial statements in compliance with Section 47 of the IPSAS No. 24 to enable the Municipality to better discharge its accountability obligations.

We recommended that Management revisit its procurement procedures for the purchase of non-bulk POL products, and consider availing the alternative mode of procurement of Direct Retail Purchase of Petroleum Fuel, Oil and Lubricant (POL) Products under Section 53.14 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, as amended, to achieve the most reasonable and advantageous prices.

We recommended that the Management require the MDC through the planning officer to include in their plans and programs projects that have an impact on health and environmental issues to control the spread of the virus; provide additional allocation for PPAs for Covid-19 preventive measures in accordance with Item 3.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01; and continue to be compliant in the preparation of COVID-19 related PPAs and assure inclusion thereof in the AIP in accordance with Section 2.1 DBM-DILG JMC No. 02.

We recommended that the management continue their effort in determining the cause of the variance of P77,528.62 and immediately effect adjustments/corrections on the noted deficiencies to reconcile the balances of the amount in the financial statements and the cashbooks.

We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Request copies of Credit Memos from the Bank and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office to record the reconciling items; and
Notify the payees/check holders one month before the check will become stale;
The Municipal Accountant to:
Draw a journal entry voucher to record in the books of accounts the reconciling items upon receipt of the necessary supporting documents; and
Record adjustments in the books of accounts to revert back the stale checks classified as outstanding checks in the BRS.

We recommended that management:
Require the Accounting Office to record first the purchases of drugs and medicines in the appropriate inventory account/s, and recognize the corresponding expenses for actual issuances to the units; and
Require the conduct of physical count of inventories semestrally and accomplish the required RPCI to be submitted to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

We recommended that management require:
The inventory committee to prepare and submit the approved PIP to the Office of the Auditor at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory count and perform the count on all PPE items as indicated in the PIP; and
The Municipal Accountant to adjust the PPE accounts by drawing the necessary Journal Entry Voucher to correct the recording of small value items.

We recommended that management direct:
The GSO and the Municipal Agriculturist’s Office to update their records by conducting a periodic physical inventory of all animals owned by the Municipality and furnishing a copy to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant adhere to the provisions of IPSAS 27 by updating the breeding stocks balance and record the additions, reductions, gains and losses through sale, distribution, and changes in fair market values at every reporting period in order to accurately report the carrying values at year-end.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Recognize liability for the taxes and contributions withheld as of year-end by debiting Due to BIR account to avoid an abnormal balance in the book of accounts; and
Continue the efforts in determining the respective causes of the abnormal balances in Due to Other LGUs, Other Payables, Due to National Government Agency and Due to Other Funds accounts and immediately effect necessary adjustments as may be warranted to ensure fairness in the presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Cause the immediate full liquidation of outstanding fund transfers received from National Government Agencies and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 6 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and Section 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012;
Collate records to ensure that all unspent balances of fund transfers for completed projects irrespective of source agency be returned; and
Fast track the implementation of the fund transfers since the resources are readily available in order not to deprive the Municipality of the benefits out of the PPAs.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the funds from the General Fund to the Trust Fund and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry vouchers (JEVs) to record the fund transfers to the appropriate books.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the PS appropriations and actual expenses for one fiscal year shall not exceed the required 55% PS limitation;
Exert effort in reducing its PS expenses by identifying and funding only PS items that are of priority concerns so that it can continue to allocate adequate funds for the implementation of other planned and development projects that are beneficial to its constituents; and
Exert effort in raising revenues to fund its additional PS costs.

We recommended and management agreed to submit copies of MOA/Trust Agreement with the SAs for the implementation of the above-mentioned projects. Henceforth, attach copies of the MOA on the disbursement vouchers to support the transactions.

We recommended and management agreed to ensure that brand names and complete detailed specifications of the items procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended and the Local Development Council agreed to adopt a system that will ensure close monitoring of the execution of all PPAs to warrant their efficient and timely completion in order to deliver the desirable socio-economic and environmental development due to the public. Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects.

We recommended and the Local School Board agreed to maximize the utilization of the SEF through implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF Budget and incorporate allowable expenses to support the BE-LCP as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020. Further for both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile discrepancies noted in the reports they prepared and submitted.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare annually the LDRRMFIP and its revisions for the DRRM program in the prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 which will include activities to be funded from the unexpended QRF and DRRMF-MOOE of the previous years which were transferred to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended and management agreed on the following:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records in order to determine the exact unexpended balance of the LDRRMF;
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer classify the LDRRM appropriations as to their allotment class; and
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries to reflect the correct balance for the payable account. Only the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE unexpended/unobligated balances be transferred to the Trust Fund while The LDRRMF for CO is to be made continuing in the General Fund until the projects are completed.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer and the MDRRMC to monitor the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the public receive the benefits that may accrue from the utilization of the fund within the targeted timelines.

The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Economic Enterprise and Development Office Head periodically provide  the  Municipal  Accountant  with  the  List  of Delinquent Market Stallholders with amount due and penalties to facilitate timely and accurate recording of accrued rental income;

The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer together with the MEEDO Manager reconcile entries in the General ledger and the MTO records at least on a quarterly basis and trace the variance of P85,194.75 and adjust accordingly to reflect the correct balance;

c.	The Municipal Accountant make the necessary correcting/adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts and furnish the Auditor a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the adjustments.

We recommended that the LGU’s accounting unit reconcile the discrepancies noted and make the necessary adjustments/corrections, as necessary, in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds.

a.	exercise prudence in the preparation of the Lapsing Schedule by completely listing all items of PPE, indicating their accurate acquisition costs, and disclosing specific identification of the items for accurate computation of depreciation and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous recording of depreciation;
b.	reconcile the variances noted in the aforementioned PPE accounts and correspondingly make the necessary adjustments to present fairly the balances in the financial statements;
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant disclose the notes related to Road Network Systems and PPE in accordance with   COA Circular No. 2015-008 and Item 88 of IPSAS 17, respectively.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 pertaining to the posting of warranty securities for completed and accepted infrastructure projects, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects.

We recommended that management comply with the procurement process in accordance with the IRR of R.A. 9184 in order to ensure the most advantageous price and generate income from sale of bid documents.

We recommended that the management establish specific set of guidelines in the granting of financial assistance to its constituents to ensure efficient implementation of the program and fund utilization. The said guidelines may include among others, the following:
Qualifications of the recipients which may be determined by the MSWDO to include class and income levels.
Category of ailments, in the case of medical assistance, such as ordinary, medical or surgical cases.
Maximum amount of financial assistance for each category.
Maximum number of days to process and release the financial assistance.
e) Frequency or maximum number of entitlements in a year.

We recommended that management post the report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project/Activity Implementation in the designated areas and send a written notice to the DBM, House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, Senate Committee on Finance, as prescribed.

Direct the GAD Focal Point System to establish sex-disaggregated data for its own personnel, conduct gender analysis based on the data established, identify organization-focused gender issues and the appropriate GAD-related activities to address the same, and incorporate them in the GPB for costing;

b) 	Require the heads/representatives of the implementing offices to ensure that GAD-related activities are implemented as planned, and maximize the utilization of the GAD funds in order to attain the objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that management direct the Liquidating Officer to physically inspect the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors; and the collectors to present daily all unused accountable forms remaining in their custody to determine their exact daily accountability.

We recommended that the collecting officers issue in chronological order the pre-numbered AF 51, or all other forms of official receipts for that matter.  In preparing the AF 51, all copies shall be exact copies or carbon reproductions in all respects of the original.  It shall reflect the date, payor, nature of collection and amount of payment received.  Further, the Municipal Treasurer shall implement strict verification of both the used and unused accountable forms to assure the accurate report of collections and deposits made by the collectors.

We recommended that the collecting officers properly and correctly record all collections made in the Cashbook.  Further, for the Treasurer or Liquidating Officer to verify the RCD; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and check the same against the new balances on hand column.  He shall indicate his verification by affixing his signature at the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued.  He shall count the money turned over to him and sign the certification and receipt portion of all copies of RCD.

We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions of Sections 3.06 and 3.07 of Market Code of the Municipality of Josefina.  Consider conducting investigation on the apparent subleasing and alteration of market stalls and enforce revocation of lease contracts, if warranted. For those who wish to transfer their rights and privileges over the stalls/blocks to the current occupants, rules on transfer of rights and privilege, as prescribed in the Market Code, must be followed so that collections of the necessary fees can be made.

We recommended that management execute contract of lease with duly qualified stallholders and ensure that the contract of lease are duly notarized before their occupation. Also, properly assign appropriate numbers to stall/block and table in Building No. 2 of the Public Market to avoid confusion in the implementation of Contracts of Lease.

We recommended that the Local Development Council review the programs and projects funded by the 20 per cent Economic Development Fund and incorporate projects in the Annual Investment Plan that are within the scope allowable. Further, for the council to monitor the implementation of the projects to achieve an optimal utilization of the Development Fund and deliver the desirable socio-economic and environmental development due to the public. Furthermore, the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the departments concerned to fast track the implementation of the projects.
We recommended that the Municipal School Board review the programs/activities funded by SEF and see to it that this has been fully maximized and achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile entries in their respective cashbook and general ledger at least once in every quarter; trace and identify the variance of P76,497.44 and provide the necessary adjustment/s to reflect the correct balances.

The Municipal Treasurer shall exercise the diligence of a good father of a family in overseeing accountable officers under her supervision, otherwise she shall be jointly and solidarily liable with them for the loss of government funds or property under their control.

10.5 We recommended that the collecting officers issue in chronological order the pre-numbered AF 51, or all other forms of official receipts for that matter.  In preparing the AF 51, all copies shall be exact copies or carbon reproductions in all respects of the original.  It shall reflect the date, payor, nature of collection and amount of payment received.  Further, the Municipal Treasurer shall implement strict verification of both the used and unused accountable forms to assure the accurate report of collections and deposits made by the collectors.

11.4 We recommended that the collecting officers properly and correctly record all collections made in the Cashbook.  Further, for the Treasurer or Liquidating Officer to verify the RCD; check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand; make a physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and check the same against the new balances on hand column.  He shall indicate his verification by affixing his signature at the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued.  He shall count the money turned over to him and sign the certification and receipt portion of all copies of RCD.

14.6 We recommended that management depart from transactions with business establishments that may have any pecuniary interests, whether directly or indirectly, of its officers and employees pursuant to R.A. 6173 and P.D. 1445.

16.6 We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions of Sections 3.06 and 3.07 of Market Code of the Municipality of Josefina.  Consider conducting investigation on the apparent subleasing and alteration of market stalls and enforce revocation of lease contracts, if warranted. For those who wish to transfer their rights and privileges over the stalls/blocks to the current occupants, rules on transfer of rights and privilege, as prescribed in the Market Code, must be followed so that collections of the necessary fees can be made.

17.6 We recommended that management execute contract of lease with duly qualified stallholders and ensure that the contract of lease are duly notarized before their occupation. Also, properly assign appropriate numbers to stall/block and table in Building No. 2 of the Public Market to avoid confusion in the implementation of Contracts of Lease.

reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated; 3.)  demand

accounts.

to ensure the most advantageous price and generate income from sale  of  bid
documents.

We recommend that management ensure that the procurement of common-use
supplies be made from the Procurement Service, in compliance  with  RA  9184
and Administrative Order No. 17. In cases where the requested items are  not
available in the PS-Depot, secure a certification from the PS-DBM that  such
items are not available.

We recommend that the  guidelines  in  the  procurement  of  drugs  and
medicines as presented in the Revised Implementing Guidelines for  the  PNFS
be strictly followed and implemented.  Hence, drugs and medicines which  are
not in the list of the PNF manual be not included in  the  procurement.   If
the said items were proven effective and there is really a need  to  procure
the same, the concerned Municipal Health Officer make  a  request  following
the revised guidelines for the inclusion of the items in  the  upcoming  8th
edition of the PNF manual.

We recommend that:   1.)  Specification  on  relevant  characteristics
and/or performance  requirements  be  provided  as  well  as  the  necessary
information be filled-up in the purchase requests; and 2.)  Strictly  comply
the observance of non-reference to brand names.

It is recommended that  management  impose  warranty  security  to  be
posted by the contractors on completed  infrastructure  projects  after  the
issuance  of  Certificate  of  Final  Acceptance  following  the  prescribed
schedule in Section 62.2.3.3. of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

We recommend that management:  1.) Provide notice of deliveries within
twenty four (24) hours from deliveries of  goods/items  delivered;  and  2.)
Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty four (24) hours.

We recommend that persons responsible  in  the  issuance  of  Purchase
Orders for the municipality include all the pertinent data  as  required  in
COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996.

We recommend that the management:  1.) Refrain  from  recognizing  the
fund transfers as  outright  expenses  without  proof  of  liquidation.  The
appropriate liability account must  be  recognized  upon  granting  of  fund
transfer; 2.) Make the  necessary  entries  on  the  books  to  correct  the
misstated  balances;  and  3.)  Require  the  concerned   NGAs   to   submit
liquidation reports of fund transferred pertaining to activities which  were
already completed for verification and  proper  accounting  thereof  and  to
refund the unused fund/balances, if any, to the municipality.

We recommend that the salaries/wages of  casual/contractual  employees
under the Contracts of Service and Job Order Appointments must be  submitted
to the Civil Service Commission,  Provincial  Office  for  review  and  also
submit the necessary supporting documents in the  payment  of  salaries  and
wages as provided  in  COA  Circular  No.  2012-001  dated  June  14,  2012,
Prescribing the Revised Guidelines and Documentary Requirements  for  Common
Government Transactions.  Reclassify to the  proper  expense  account  those
salaries and wages of casuals/contractuals which were  charged  to  Salaries
and Wages – Regular and the Consultancy Services.

We  recommend  that  the  management:   1.)  Refrain   from   granting
additional allowances or other  forms  of  compensation  to  any  government
official or employees, except those  specifically  authorized  by  law;  2.)
Procure the food/meals to be  served  to  the  participants  during  various
meetings, conferences, celebrations and  trainings  in  accordance  with  RA
9184  and  refrain  from  granting  food  allowances  as  there  is  no  law
authorizing such payments; and 3.) Desist from charging  food  expenses  for
office consumption against the LGU’s appropriations.  Such  expenses  should
be charged against the Representation Allowance granted the Municipal  Mayor
and the Sanguniang Bayan members.

It is recommended that management prepare  and  submit  the  following
reports for verification and validation,  to  wit:   1.)  LDRRMF  Investment
Plan;  and  2.)  Report  on  Utilization  of  Disaster  Risk  Reduction  and
Management Fund (Annex B) and Notes to Financial  Statements  (Annex  D)  as
required in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management refrain  from  charging  expenditures  of
items that are not covered in the circular & the law  created  in  order  to
promulgate  the  prioritized  projects,  programs  &   activities   in   the
attainment of disaster risk reduction management.

It is recommended that management fully comply with the role of the LGU
as provided for under the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of  2000  to
have a systematic, comprehensive and ecological waste management program.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we  recommend  that
the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  and  other
applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD  Plan  and
Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment  Reports  to  be
approved by the different  offices  concerned  and  expenditures  should  be
limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  that
all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) Attend GAD  related
seminars  and  trainings  to  keep  abreast  with  updates  and   build   up
competencies in the execution of  GAD  related  laws  and  regulations;  3.)
Gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information  that
can be sourced in the agencies  suggested  by  the  Circular;  Maintain  and
establish GAD database to serve as  basis  for  gender-responsive  planning,
programming and policy formulation; and 4.) Assist formulation  and  passage
of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting,  protecting  and
fulfilling women’s human rights,  women  economic  empowerment  and  gender-
responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and  women’s
empowerment.

The GAD Focal Point System should gather and build up sex-disaggregated
data and GAD related information and maintain and establish GAD database to
serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy
formulation.

In view of the foregoing observations noted above, we  recommend  that
the GAD Focal Point System:  1.) Strictly  comply  with  the  provisions  of
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of  the  PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  and  other
applicable rulings relative to the submission of the approved GAD  Plan  and
Budget and the preparation of the Annual GAD Accomplishment  Reports  to  be
approved by the different  offices  concerned  and  expenditures  should  be
limited to the amount approved in the GAD Plan and  Budget  to  ensure  that
all planned projects and activities are implemented; 2.) Attend GAD  related
seminars  and  trainings  to  keep  abreast  with  updates  and   build   up
competencies in the execution of  GAD  related  laws  and  regulations;  3.)
Gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information  that
can be sourced in the agencies  suggested  by  the  Circular;  Maintain  and
establish GAD database to serve as  basis  for  gender-responsive  planning,
programming and policy formulation; and 4.) Assist formulation  and  passage
of GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting,  protecting  and
fulfilling women’s human rights,  women  economic  empowerment  and  gender-
responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and  women’s
empowerment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant comply with the  provisions
of  Section I, Rule VI of the  IRR  of  R.A.  No.  9679  on  the  amount  of
premiums for government share to be remitted to the Pag-ibig Fund.

It is recommended that the Municipal Mayor,  Municipal  Treasurer  and
Municipal  Accountant  immediately  remit  the  taxes  withheld  within  the
reglementary period as prescribed by the  rules  and  regulations  to  avoid
penalties, which  are  personal  in  nature,  and  help  contribute  to  the
collections for the operation of our national government.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 1.) prepare monthly the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same within the reglementary period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 together with its supporting documents such as Monthly Bank Statement to facilitate the early submission of the BRS, Subsidiary Ledgers, paid checks, debit and credit memos and deposit slips; and 2.) prepare the necessary adjusting entries for any reconciling items noted in the process.

It is recommended that management: 1.) refrain from granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officer with previous unliquidated cash advances; 2.)  prepare supporting schedule of Advances to Officers and Employees account to identify the accountable officers concerned and Aging of Cash Advances Schedule; 3.)  enforce full liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary period provided under COA Circular No.97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and 4.)  demand the concerned officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances to effect immediate settlement of cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement as provided for in paragraph 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 shall be enforced.
observations.reason: Cash advances still remain unliquidated

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the management refrain from granting cash advances for payments of account of infrastructure projects or other undertaking on a project basis and strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in COA Circular 97-002 in the granting and utilization of cash advances.

It is recommended that management: 1.) require the Municipal Accountant to submit the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds to enable the Auditor to conduct a timely verification and audit of the accounts of the Municipality otherwise the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and 2.) submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands.

It is recommended that management: 1.) refrain from using SVP as an alternative mode of procurement in order to evade the proper procurement procedures as required under R.A. 9184; 2.) consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner or Purchase Order (PO) based on two (2) or more requisitions for the same or about the same time by different requisitioners to avoid splitting; and 3.) conduct competitive bidding for all procurement, except when there is proof that public bidding cannot be ultimately pursued and in accordance with Rule XVI of the IRR of RA 9184.

It is recommended that: 1) payment of honoraria should be in accordance with the provisions of Sections 95 of  Republic Act 7160 and  Section 2.1 of Budget Circular No. 2007-2, and 2) Stop the payment of honoraria to officials/ employees and members of the JHCSC, Josefina, Zamboanga del Sur in the absence of any  legal basis to avoid disallowance in audit.

It is recommended that the Municipal Accountant, examine the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction before payment shall be done to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances in audit and to strictly comply with the provisions set in DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

It is  recommended that the 1)  Municipal Accountant evaluate the claims for subsistence allowance of the PHWs in conformity with the provisions of DBM and DOH Joint Circular No. 1, S. 2012; and  2)  concerned PHWs  refund the excess amount of hazard pay allowance and subsistence allowance claimed.

It is recommended that management should transfer the unexpended LDRRMF balance, except those for capital outlay, to the Trust Fund bank account and record the same in the Trust Fund books as “Trust Liabilities – DRRM Fund”.

We recommend that management should:

 Provide notice of deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from deliveries of goods/items delivered; and
 Submit Report of Inspection of Consumables within twenty four (24) hours.
